name: John Wu
headline: CS PhD Student at UIUC · AI for Healthcare
bio: Hello! My name is John Wu, a Ph.D. student in the Computer Science Department at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, advised by Professor Jimeng Sun. I am supported by the NSF GRFP.

My research focuses on the transparent deployment of AI systems in healthcare, spanning model interpretability, agentic systems for clinical workflows, and open-source healthcare AI development toolkits. I believe that for AI to be safely adopted in high-stakes clinical settings, it must be both effective and explainable.

I also lead the PyHealth Discord community (400+ members) and run the PyHealth Research Initiative — a program of 10+ researchers actively conducting open-source, reproducible healthcare AI research built on top of PyHealth.

projects.name: PyHealth
projects.description: An open-source Python toolkit for clinical deep learning, unifying 15+ datasets, 20+ clinical tasks, and 25+ models across EHR, signals, and imaging modalities. PyHealth 2.0 features up to 39× faster processing, 20× reduced memory usage, conformal prediction for uncertainty quantification, and multiple interpretability methods. I maintain a 400+ member Discord community around PyHealth and lead the PyHealth Research Initiative — a program of 10+ researchers conducting open-source, reproducible healthcare AI research.

experience.organization: Sun Lab, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
Rerout Lab, The Ohio State University
